title: On Clifford hierarchy testing and near-extremizers of noncommutative uniformity norms
authors: Zongbo, Bao, Jop Briët, Davi Castro-Silva, Philippe van Dordrecht, Jonas Helsen
abstract: We consider the problem of testing whether an unknown unitary is close to a specified level of the Clifford hierarchy. Bu, Gu, and Jaffe proposed a candidate tester for this task based on a connection with noncommutative analogues of the Gowers uniformity norms. The complexity of this tester -- whose analysis depends on a robust characterization of the near-extremizers of these norms -- was left open. We establish such a characterization for the fourth noncommutative uniformity norm and, as a consequence, obtain an efficient tester for the third level of the Clifford hierarchy. We further discuss possible routes toward resolving the problem of testing for all higher levels, highlighting the main barriers that remain.
summary: This paper provides a way to efficiently test if a quantum gate belongs to the third level of the Clifford hierarchy. It achieves this by proving that if a specific mathematical norm is large, the gate must be close to the desired hierarchy level, bridging quantum information with additive combinatorics.
main_problem: The paper addresses the problem of testing whether an unknown unitary belongs to a specific level of the Clifford hierarchy and seeks to establish inverse theorems for noncommutative Pauli uniformity norms.
main_result: The authors establish a 99% inverse theorem for the fourth Pauli uniformity norm and provide an efficient algorithm (C3Tester) to distinguish unitaries close to the third level of the Clifford hierarchy.
method: The study uses higher-order Fourier analysis, inductive arguments adapted from additive combinatorics, and the construction of unitary representations of the Pauli group via Pauli derivatives.
model_or_system: The mathematical framework involves unitary operators acting on a finite-dimensional Hilbert space (n-qudits) and the algebraic structure of the Pauli group and Clifford hierarchy.
key_observables: Degree-k Clifford fidelity and the k-th Pauli uniformity norm (Pk).
important_parameters: The 99% regime (near-extremizers), the 1% regime, and the hierarchy level k.
main_assumptions: The analysis focuses on the 99% setting where the norm is close to its maximum; the extension to higher levels (k >= 5) is limited by the lack of multiplicative closure in the hierarchy.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the testing problem, defines the Pauli uniformity norms, presents the construction of the C3Tester algorithm, proves the 99% inverse theorem for k up to 4 using induction, and discusses the barriers to higher-order extensions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
